patches.rationale: ISS-1791: adds the specific code snippet and line reference from monitor.py that verifies the hardcoding claim. The predicted/observed values at line ~852 were confirmed in the 2026-05-14 poller change report extract.

patches.target_locator: WIN id='026' -> detail_verdict_text: the sentence starting 'Anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point'
patches.current_text: Anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point — the actual empirical content of the WIN — is never tested by the script; it cannot be
patches.proposed_text: The script never tests anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point — the actual empirical content of the WIN — and cannot do so
patches.rationale: ISS-1792: restructures passive 'is never tested' to active 'never tests', removes the awkward mid-sentence em-dash interruption, and leads with the agent (script) for clearer reading. Meaning unchanged.

patches.target_locator: WIN id='026' -> detail_evidence: the sentence ending 'manufacture parallel rays from a finite source.' before 'Notably,'
patches.current_text: no aetheric-refraction caveat about the 5,733 km figure can manufacture parallel rays from a finite source. Notably, the
patches.proposed_text: no aetheric-refraction caveat about the 5,733 km figure can manufacture parallel rays from a finite source. Any proposed aetheric collimation mechanism must specify a refractive-index profile capable of parallelising radial-fan rays from a localised source, preserve the observed ~0.53° apparent solar diameter, and simultaneously satisfy further independent observational constraints — an over-determined conjunction that collapses to unfalsifiable free-parameter adjustment. Notably, the
patches.rationale: ISS-1799: the prior sentence asserted without argument that no aetheric caveat can manufacture parallel rays. The added sentence provides the specific over-determination argument: (a) a refractive profile must be specified, (b) it must not distort the observed solar angular diameter, (c) it must pass other independent tests simultaneously. This triples the logical force of the assertion and directly addresses the curmudgeon's H1 finding.

tldr_evidence: monitor.py line ~852 shows predicted: '5733', observed: '5733' — the hardcoding is confirmed. Aetheric collimation is logically over-determined: specifying a profile, preserving solar diameter, and clearing independent observational constraints simultaneously is the over-determination argument that was asserted but not justified.
tldr_verdict: Three minor WIN-026 prose improvements: add code cite for verifiability, clarify active voice on script limitation, add one-sentence over-determination argument for aetheric collimation.
